Title: Husband recounts missed goodbye before wife's fatal crash
Article:
GERIK: "Every morning before leaving for work, we would shake hands and kiss each other goodbye. But yesterday, we were both in a rush and didn't get the chance."
Those heartbreaking words were all Hazrul Jalaluddin could utter as he recalled the final morning he shared with his wife, Zuraida Abu Bakar, before she was killed in a crash that also claimed the life of a police inspector in Lenggong yesterday.
Hazrul, 45, said his wife hurried out of their home to attend a course at Kolej Kemahiran Tinggi Mara (KKTM) Lenggong, while he was rushing to a meeting in Ipoh.
Neither of them knew it would be the last time they would see each other.
Zuraida, 47, a teacher at Gerik Vocational College, was among six teachers travelling in a Honda Odyssey when it collided with a Toyota Vios at Km29 of the Kuala Kangsar-Gerik-Pengkalan Hulu Road, near a restaurant here.
She died at the scene together with Inspector Melvindar Singh Gamum Singh, 41, of the Pengkalan Hulu district police headquarters. Five other teachers travelling with Zuraida were injured.
Speaking outside the Forensic Unit of Gerik Hospital today, Hazrul said he first learnt of the tragedy through a telephone call from a teacher while attending a meeting in Ipoh.
He recalled that his wife had behaved differently in the days leading up to the tragedy.
"Our routine was simple. Whether she left first or I did, we would always shake hands and kiss before going to work. But yesterday, she rushed out because she wanted to carpool with a friend.
"For the past week, I had this strange feeling that I was going to lose her. Even her expression looked different, especially when she was asleep.
"I love going on motorcycle rides and she would usually refuse, saying she was too tired. But lately, whenever I asked, she agreed without hesitation, even when it looked like it was going to rain," he said.
The father of three said the loss had devastated the family, particularly their youngest child, a 12-year-old daughter who was especially close to her mother.
"We have been married for 20 years. Just last month, we celebrated our 20th wedding anniversary.
"Our youngest is very attached to her mother. It will be the hardest for her," said Hazrul, an assistant agricultural officer with the Rubber Industry Smallholders Development Authority (Risda).
Family members, relatives and teachers from Gerik Vocational College gathered at the hospital's forensic unit to pay their last respects.
Zuraida's remains will be taken to Masjid Nurussalam in Batu 4, Jalan Kuala Rui, for funeral prayers after Friday prayers before being laid to rest at the Kampung Telok Batu Islamic Cemetery in Lenggong.
Meanwhile, Inspector Melvindar's remains will be managed by the Pengkalan Hulu district police headquarters before being sent to his family in Klang, Selangor.
Two of the five injured teachers remain in serious condition and are receiving treatment at Raja Permaisuri Bainun Hospital in Ipoh and Taiping Hospital.
Police said the six teachers were returning from a
course
at KKTM Lenggong when the collision occurred.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Anwar: Govt to build more housing for civil servants
Article:
DENGKIL: The government will build more housing for civil servants on government-owned land at lower rental rates to help ease their cost of living burden, Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said.
Anwar said the move followed rising rental rates that have affected civil servants, particularly in major cities.
"During my recent visits to Penang, Perak, Negri Sembilan, Johor and Kuala Lumpur, I found that civil servants are facing a serious problem — rising rental rates.
"Even though we have raised their salaries by between 15 per cent and a maximum of 30 per cent, sometimes in places like Johor Baru, Kuala Lumpur, Seremban and Ipoh, it is still not enough to cover the rising cost of rent," he said after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Jameatus Solehah in Dengkil today.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Boy, 12, caught after six-hour expressway drive, claimed to be adult with dwarfism
Article:
BEIJING: A 12-year-old boy in southern China was detained after allegedly driving his neighbour's car alone on an expressway for more than six hours before claiming he was an adult with dwarfism when stopped by police.
The incident occurred on June 15 after toll staff at Gaotian Toll Station in Guangxi Zhuang Autonomous Region became suspicious when the boy said he did not have enough money to pay the toll, according to the South China Morning Post, citing Guilin Traffic Police.
Staff alerted police after noticing the driver appeared far too small to be behind the wheel.
When questioned, the boy, surnamed Luo, insisted he was an adult with dwarfism. Police later confirmed he was 12 years old and had no driving licence.
Investigators said Luo allegedly took his neighbour's car keys about 4am and entered the G65 Baomao Expressway via Xiangshan Toll Station in Guilin around 4.45am.
He then drove through several counties and remained on the expressway for more than six hours before stopping at Gaotian Toll Station after running out of money to pay the toll.
Luo initially told officers he was visiting his grandmother and claimed the car belonged to his aunt.
He also said it was his first time driving and that he had learned during the journey.
Police later confirmed the vehicle belonged to a neighbour, who was unaware it had been taken until contacted by officers.
Luo later admitted he and several friends had secretly taught themselves to drive for fun.
As he is under 14, police have summoned his guardian and the vehicle owner to assist in investigations.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Isa Samad to remain in jail
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Former Felda chairman Datuk Seri Mohd Isa Abdul Samad will remain in jail after the Federal Court dismissed his bid to review its earlier decision reinstating his conviction in a corruption case involving RM3.09 million linked to the purchase of Merdeka Palace Hotel & Suites in Kuching, Sarawak.
A three-member Federal Court review panel chaired by Chief Judge of Sabah and Sarawak Datuk Azizah Nawawi ruled that Isa's application had no merit, finding that his right to be heard and to appeal had already been afforded in the earlier apex court proceedings.
On Feb 10, the Federal Court unanimously reinstated Isa's conviction and sentence after allowing the prosecution's appeal against the Court of Appeal's decision to acquit him on March 6, 2024.
The 77-year-old former Negeri Sembilan menteri besar is serving his sentence at Sungai Buloh Prison.
MORE TO COME
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Search continues for fisherman missing after fall overboard near Pulau Kendi
Article:
GEORGE TOWN: A 51-year-old fisherman is missing after he is believed to have fallen overboard from a fishing boat near Pulau Kendi, Batu Maung, prompting a multi-agency search and rescue operation.
Southwest district police chief Assistant Commissioner Anuwal Ab Wahab said police received a report on the incident at 8.53am yesterday involving crewman Tang Chin Khoon.
He said Tang was one of four crew members aboard a fishing boat engaged in fishing activities near Pulau Kendi.
Initial investigations showed the boat had departed about 6pm on June 24.
"While returning around 10pm, the skipper realised Tang was no longer onboard and believed he had fallen into the sea near Pulau Kendi.
"The skipper and nearby fishing boats immediately conducted a search but failed to locate him," he said in a statement today.
Anuwal said the Marine Police, Malaysian Maritime Enforcement Agency (MMEA) and other agencies had since
launched a coordinated search and rescue (SAR) operation
.
Fishermen and vessels operating in the area have also been alerted to assist in the search.
He said there were no elements of foul play detected so far, and the case is being classified as a missing person at sea pending the outcome of the SAR operation.
Members of the public with information on the incident or the victim's whereabouts are urged to contact the southwest district police headquarters or the nearest police station.
"Police will continue working closely with all agencies involved and will provide updates as developments arise," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Bird's nest firm Enest unveils IPO prospectus for ACE listing
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Bird's nest processor and trader Enest Group Bhd has launched its prospectus in conjunction with its initial public offering (IPO) and proposed transfer from the Leap Market to the ACE Market of Bursa Malaysia.
The IPO involves a public issue of 116.25 million new ordinary shares, representing 20 per cent of the company's enlarged issued share capital of 581.25 million shares, as well as an offer for sale of 15.05 million existing shares, or about 2.6 per cent of the enlarged capital.
At an IPO price of 13 sen per share, the public issue is expected to raise gross proceeds of RM15.11 million for the company, while proceeds from the offer for sale will go entirely to selling shareholders.
Enest said proceeds from the public issue will be used to repay bank borrowings (RM5 million), for working capital (RM6.41 million), and to defray listing expenses (RM3.7 million).
Tracing its roots to 2015, the company has grown into an established player in the edible bird's nest industry, focusing on processing and sale of raw, clean edible bird's nest, trading activities, and processed bird's nest products.
Its customers include distributors, importers, processing companies and retailers, with China as its largest market. Beyond its core business, Enest also produces bottled bird's nest and health beverages and operates a health and personal care retail business under the "Kang Li" brand.
Looking ahead, Enest plans to strengthen its growth through the establishment of a new facility in Kajang to produce bottled bird's nest and herbal beverages, with an annual capacity of about 300,000 bottles.
The company also aims to expand its customer base in China, particularly among traditional Chinese medicine and wellness product manufacturers, while broadening its export activities to include raw, unclean bird's nest.
Enest managing director Tan Teh Jie said that since the company's early beginnings, it has steadily expanded its capabilities, customer reach and market presence within the edible bird's nest industry.
"Through this IPO, we aim to strengthen our balance sheet, enhance our working capital position and support the next phase of growth for our business," he said in a statement.
M&A Equity Holdings Bhd managing director Datuk Bill Tan said Enest has built an established position within Malaysia's edible bird's nest industry through its processing capabilities, export experience and access to the Chinese market.
"With its presence across multiple segments of the edible bird's nest value chain, the company is well-positioned to pursue its next phase of growth as a company listed on the ACE Market," he said.
M&A Securities Sdn Bhd is the adviser, sponsor, underwriter, and placement agent for this IPO.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Police hunt man who attacked two cops during arrest
Article:
SELAMA: Police are hunting for a 39-year-old man who allegedly assaulted two policemen trying to arrest him on Tuesday.
District police chief Superintendent Ramzi Mahmudin said the suspect, who is believed to be a drug addict, remains at large, and efforts to track him down are ongoing.
"Not yet (arrested). Once he is arrested, a
statement will be issued by the Perak police chief (Datuk Mohd Alwi Zainal Abidin)," he said briefly.
When asked about the condition of the two policemen, he said both had been discharged from Taiping Hospital.
Mohd Alwi had previously said police received information at about noon on Tuesday regarding a man believed to be a drug addict who was at a hut beside an unnumbered house in Kampung Sungai Jernih, Ijok.
Acting on the information, he said police conducted surveillance at the location before moving in to arrest the suspect.
"During the arrest, the suspect turned aggressive and attacked the police officers with a sharp weapon believed to be a knife before fleeing the scene.
"In the incident, two police personnel, a 44-year-old sergeant and a 27-year-old constable sustained injuries during a struggle with the suspect and were taken to hospital for treatment," he said.
Following the incident, Mohd Alwi said police had intensified efforts to locate and apprehend the suspect.
"Various measures have been implemented, including the establishment of roadblocks at entry and exit points around the area to track the suspect's movements," he said.
The case is being investigated under Section 307 of the Penal Code for attempted murder, which carries a prison sentence of up to 10 years upon conviction.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 23 Rolex watches, 15 jewellery items forfeited to govt
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Twenty-three Rolex watches and 15 pieces of jewellery seized by the Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) from the sister-in-law of former army chief General (Rtd) Tan Sri Muhammad Hafizuddeain Jantan have been forfeited to the government.
The sister-in-law was identified as Nor Shahirah Athirah Anuar @ Kamaruddin.
Deputy public prosecutor Mahadi Abdul Jumaat said Sessions Court judge Rosli Ahmad made the ruling today after no third party came forward to claim the assets.
"The court has allowed the prosecution's application for forfeiture. However, no third party came forward to claim the assets," he said.
Previously, MACC had sought a court order to publish a gazette notice calling on any party with interest in the assets to come forward under Section 41(2) of the MACC Act 2009.
On May 4, the prosecution filed the forfeiture application against Nor Shahirah, seeking to seize the 23 Rolex watches and 15 pieces of jewellery.
The application was made under Section 41(1) of the MACC Act, read together with Section 376 of the Criminal Procedure Code, on the grounds that the assets were subject to forfeiture as no criminal prosecution had been initiated under the Act.
On Jan 10, the New Straits Times reported that MACC found items worth RM2.2 million in two suitcases during a raid on the woman's home.
Sources said the 23 Rolex watches, comprising various models, were estimated to be worth RM2 million, while the seized jewellery, valued at RM225,000, belonged to a person suspected of being an accomplice.
MACC Investigation Division senior director Datuk Zainul Darus confirmed the matter.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Sony discontinues Japan sales of robot puppy 'aibo'
Article:
TOKYO: Sony is halting sales of its robotic puppy "aibo" in Japan, the company said, eight years after the latest model of its interactive android pet became an instant hit.
The announcement marks the end of an era for loyal fans of the high-tech toy, which develops its own personality and can perform tricks like waving and mimicking its owner.
The ERS-1000 aibo model – a 30-centimetre (one-foot) long hound with flapping ears, eyes that show emotions, and a nose camera – charmed customers on its launch in 2018, clocking up 20,000 sales in the first six months.
It was also a big comeback for Sony's robot dog.
The earliest iteration of aibo came out in 1999, followed by numerous different models, from angular silver bots to more cuddly round-faced versions.
More than 150,000 units sold during the period until 2006, when Sony, facing a tough business environment, pulled the plug on the canine gadget, seen as something of a frivolous luxury.
Sony said in a statement Thursday that Japan sales of the current ERS-1000 model will be discontinued once stock runs out.
Services such as access to tech support, replacement parts and subscriptions to online cloud plans to store the robodog's memories will be maintained, it said.
When asked about plans for a potential new generation of aibo, Sony told AFP that "the aibo business will continue."
"While we are unable to provide any specific details regarding future product plans at this stage, we will continue to expand our range of new products and services so that aibo remains a partner cherished by its owners and continues to grow alongside them," it said.
Sony declined to give a total sales figure for the aibo model released in 2018.
But it said that sales will continue in the United States, where aibo retails for more than $3,000. It is not sold in any other countries.
Japanese fans had a vocal reaction to the news.
"I've been in a daze ever since I saw the announcement. Until more information comes out, I need to be careful not to mess up my daily life from overthinking," wrote X user Yachi.
"So it's over already? It didn't last until the golden age of physical AI," Yusuke Ando wrote.
Another X user called Daiyamondo had a suggestion for Sony: "Next time you develop one, could you make it half the size, and cat-shaped?"
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Samsung readies US$648bil bet, as AI boom reshapes South Korea
Article:
SEOUL: Samsung Group plans on Monday to pledge investment of 1,000 trillion won (US$648 billion) in South Korea over 10 years, part of an initiative that aims to turn the country's AI boom into a nationwide growth engine, a media report said.
Top executives from Samsung Electronics and SK Hynix — firms that have reaped huge profits as AI drives relentless demand for chips — will attend a meeting with President Lee Jae Myung and lay out investment plans targeting regions beyond Seoul, the Maeil Business Newspaper said on Friday.
Samsung's investment will include spending on AI data centres, batteries and displays as well as a potential 300 trillion won push to build chip factories in the country's southwest, it said without citing sources.
The concentration of the chipmakers' production facilities in areas around Seoul has long drawn political pressure and has been amplified by Lee's push for balanced regional development.
Opposition lawmakers say the plan is politically driven, accusing the government of pressuring companies to invest in the ruling party's southwestern stronghold ahead of the party's leadership contest.
The presidential office said it will unveil "three mega-projects" on Monday to drive a national leap forward. Policy adviser Kim Yong-beom added that the plans — spanning semiconductors, AI data centres and robotics — will be outlined jointly by government and industry, with significant investment expected.
Samsung and SK Hynix declined to comment.
INVESTING BEYOND SEOUL
South Korea has been a central player in and beneficiary of the global AI wave as it dominates global manufacturing of high-end memory chips that are crucial components in AI data centres.
Samsung Group is South Korea's biggest conglomerate, with chip giant Samsung Electronics as its crown jewel. Other big firms in the group include battery maker Samsung SDI and IT services company Samsung SDS.
Kim has said that SK Hynix and Samsung may need to bring forward projects slated for the 2040s to the mid-2030s because AI-driven memory demand was growing faster than expected, leaving no room, power or water in the capital region for expansion.
Further concentration of manufacturing capacity around Seoul risks inflating property prices and widening inequality, he said.
However, some experts question the wisdom of embarking on a chip hub in the southwest.
Securing skilled workers will be extremely difficult in the southwest, "and that will determine whether the project succeeds or fails", said Kim Tae-yun, a professor of administration at Hanyang University.
"Unless a truly cutting-edge fab is built, the local economic impact will be limited — it risks becoming little more than a construction project and a real estate boost."
REGIONAL DEVELOPMENT
The regional politics surrounding semiconductor investment became a flashpoint ahead of South Korea's June 3 local elections, and debate over where the next wave of funding should go has intensified as Lee's government has made AI a core economic policy priority.
Lee's approval rating has fallen to 51 per cent, the lowest since his inauguration in June last year, Gallup Korea said on Friday.
Candidates across multiple regions have aggressively pitched their areas as the next semiconductor hub. Proposals ranged from a 500 trillion won chip complex in the southwest to expanded clusters in some regions, according to local media.
The debate has also stirred concern in existing chipmaking cities such as Icheon, where SK Hynix operates major plants and local finances are heavily tied to the company.
"Most of the city's tax revenue comes from SK's chip plant, and our welfare depends on it," said Jo Jun-taek, head of a grassroots group in Icheon.
"If a new cluster is created, we think SK will likely cut output here and eventually close the plant. That would cause an outflow of people — the city would become a ghost town."
Lee has promoted a plan to establish "five regional hubs and three special self-governing provinces" to counterbalance the dominance of the Seoul area, which accounted for 52.8 per cent of South Korea's gross regional domestic product in 2024.
The disparity is particularly evident in Gwangju — a key southwestern city with one of the country's smaller regional economies and below-average per-capita output, according to official data.
Local media have reported that Samsung Electronics is considering Gwangju as a potential investment site.
Lee won 49.42 per cent of the national vote in the June 2025 presidential election but secured about 85 per cent in Gwangju and neighbouring South Jeolla, election data showed.
The main opposition People Power Party has accused the administration of politicising semiconductor investment.
"Where semiconductor factories are built should be decided by companies, not by the president," PPP spokesperson Park Sung-hoon said this week.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Caltex brings STEM education drive to Perak
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Caltex Malaysia has expanded its science, technology, engineering and mathematics (STEM) focused education initiative, the Fuel Your School (FYS) programme, to Perak.
This is part of the initiative's 11th edition, continuing Caltex Malaysia's push to strengthen science learning and food sustainability in underfunded schools.
The 2026 rollout covers 10 selected schools in Perak and builds on earlier implementations in Johor and Kelantan, where the programme introduced aquaponics-based learning systems as part of its broader STEM education efforts.
Implemented in collaboration with MyKasih Foundation and HAVVA Agrotech, and endorsed by the Education Ministry, the programme integrates smart aquaponics systems into school learning environments.
The initiative also aims to promote food security awareness through hands-on agricultural experience.
According to Caltex Malaysia, the expansion to Perak marks a natural progression of the programme following earlier successes in southern and east coast states, with the state previously participating in 2017.
Each participating school will receive a smart aquaponics system featuring automated water circulation designed to create a self-sustaining farming ecosystem.
HAVVA Agrotech will also conduct food security workshops for students and teachers to ensure proper system management and long-term sustainability.
To further enhance community involvement, each school has been paired with a local Caltex service station retail partner, which will provide additional support through workshops, events and ongoing engagement beyond financial contributions.
Since its launch in 2012, Fuel Your School has benefited more than 112,000 individuals nationwide.
Earlier aquaponics-focused editions in Johor and Kelantan have collectively produced around 1,235 kilogrammes (kg) of vegetables and 210kg of fish over a two-year period.
Caltex Malaysia said the initiative reflects its long-term commitment to community development, particularly in improving access to quality STEM education while promoting sustainability through practical, real-world learning experiences.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Malaysia retains top spot as Muslim-friendly travel destination for 11th straight year
Article:
SINGAPORE: When it comes to halal travel, most Malaysians would first think of overseas destinations like Indonesia, Turkey or Saudi Arabia.
However, unbeknownst to many, Malaysia is actually the world's leading Muslim-friendly travel destination, and has been for the past 11 years.
Malaysia boasts a safe environment, robust halal certification, ubiquitous prayer facilities and family-friendly resorts, making it a top choice among Muslim travellers.
This year, Malaysia clinched the Top Muslim-Friendly Destination (OIC) award in the Global Muslim Travel Index (GMTI) 2026 report, presented by Mastercard and CrescentRating.
Malaysia received a score of 83, four points above last year's rating.
11 YEARS AND BEYOND
During the awards ceremony, Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry deputy secretary-general Chua Choon Hwa shared about how far Malaysia had come.
"It is an honour to see this segment flourish into what it is today. When Malaysia first ventured into the Muslim travel market, it was still a foreign concept to many destinations. Today, the warmth of Muslim-friendly hospitality can be felt across continents," he said.
Chua said Malaysia's sustained success was the result of structured efforts by the Islamic Tourism Centre (ITC), an agency under the Tourism, Arts and Culture Ministry, which continues to set global benchmarks through national hospitality guidelines.
ITC director-general Mohammad Faisal Abu Suaib Khan said Malaysia's ability to retain the top ranking for 11 consecutive years validated the strength of its Islamic tourism framework.
"Since ITC's establishment in 2009, we have focused on research and industry development to understand the market. Today, destinations are looking to our MFTH framework as a trusted roadmap for serving the global Muslim travel market," he said.
STRONG LOCAL SUPPORT
Malaysia's success is supported by strong collaboration among agencies, including the Department of Islamic Development Malaysia (Jakim), the Department of Standards Malaysia and the Housing and Local Government Ministry.
Through supportive policies, planning frameworks and local development requirements, facilities such as prayer spaces, qibla direction indicators and halal-certified food offerings have been integrated into the visitor experience.
A key component of the ecosystem is the Muslim-Friendly Tourism and Hospitality Assurance and Recognition (MFAR) and Muslim-Friendly Tour Guide (MFTG) programmes.
Recently, about 60 wellness operators from the Association of Malaysian Spas and the Malaysian Association of Wellness & Spa underwent MFAR competency training, marking the first such initiative for the spa and wellness sector.
The programme introduced Organisation of Islamic Cooperation (OIC)-aligned guidelines, operational procedures such as Sertu and Luqatah, and promoted Urutan Malaysia as a distinctive national therapeutic experience.
Hotels, tour guides, travel agencies, attractions, transport operators and small and medium enterprises participating in these programmes form the backbone of Malaysia's Muslim-friendly tourism framework.
GOING GLOBAL
Malaysia is also expanding its expertise internationally.
"Some of the strongest interest in MFAR is coming from non-OIC countries like Russia and Taiwan because they understand that assurance is the key to attracting Muslim travellers.
"The growing international adoption of MFAR is a reflection of Malaysia's credibility in developing practical and trusted Muslim-friendly tourism standards," said Mohammad Faisal.
In May, ITC established a strategic alliance with Russia's leading halal assurance body, RusQuality (Roskachestvo), opening opportunities in the global Muslim travel market valued at US$235 billion (RM974 billion).
ITC also signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) with Taiwan Halal Certification Development Co Ltd at the Negri Sembilan International Conference on Halal and Sustainability Ecosystems.
This partnership will include outreach, training, capacity building and industry audits to enhance Taiwan's readiness in serving Muslim travellers.
Moving forward, ITC will continue providing international consultancy services in research, training and institutional capacity building.
Among the key events planned this year are Islamic Tourism Month 2026 (Sept 1-30), the 5th World Islamic Tourism Conference (Sept 17-20), and the Halal Street Food Festival and Modest Wear Expo (Oct 22-25).
These initiatives support the Malaysia Madani vision, which emphasises sustainability, inclusivity and innovation in advancing global hospitality standards.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Over 100 Buddhist police personnel join special Police Day prayers
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: More than 100 Buddhist officers and personnel from Bukit Aman and the Kuala Lumpur contingent attended a special prayer ceremony at Buddhist Maha Vihara in Brickfields today in conjunction with the 219th Police Day celebration.
The annual programme, which began at 8am, brought together serving and retired police personnel, religious leaders and invited guests to receive blessings from Buddhist Chief Priest of Malaysia Venerable Datuk Dr K. Sri Dhammaratanna.
Inspector-General of Police Tan Sri Mohd Khalid Ismail's speech was delivered by Commercial Crime Investigation Department assistant director (Forgery and Syndicated Investment Scam Investigation) Senior Assistant Commissioner Marvin Tan Seng Kian.
Speaking on behalf of Khalid, Tan said the annual ceremony reflected the solidarity of the Buddhist community within the Royal Malaysia Police while reaffirming the force's commitment to safeguarding the nation's peace and security.
He thanked Sri Dhammaratanna and the management of Buddhist Maha Vihara for their longstanding support and cooperation with the police.
Tan said places of worship were important partners in nurturing responsible citizens and strengthening social harmony.
"The values nurtured through religion serve as a strong foundation in preventing social ills and fostering a safe, peaceful and harmonious society," he said.
He added that this year's Police Day theme, "Police and Community Are Inseparable", reflected the close partnership between the force and the public in preserving national security and public order.
Tan also paid tribute to retired police personnel, saying their dedication and sacrifices would continue to inspire future generations of officers.
Also present were retired officers Datuk Kang Chez Chiang, Datuk Koh Hong Sun and Datuk Leo Chong Cheong.
The ceremony concluded with prayers for Malaysia's continued peace and stability, as well as for the safety and well-being of police personnel in carrying out their duties.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RM200mil allocated for non-Muslim houses of worship maintenance, says Nga
Article:
KLUANG: The Housing and Local Government Ministry has allocated RM200 million for the maintenance of non-Muslim houses of worship (RIBI) nationwide over a four-year period beginning 2023.
Minister Nga Kor Ming said the initiative covered maintenance works for churches, gurdwaras, Hindu temples and association premises, reflecting what he described as the Madani government's commitment to fairness across all communities.
He said the ministry had received 1,478 applications via the e-RIBI system, valued at more than RM279 million, reflecting continued demand for upgrades and maintenance of such premises.
"With the spirit of no one left behind and no one marginalised, we want to ensure that Malaysians of all backgrounds – Malay, Chinese, Indian, Iban, Kadazan-Dusun – are united as a caring and respectful family," he said.
"When there are those who build walls, we build bridges. When there are those who foster division, we foster unity."
He said this at a press conference after attending the Johor state RIBI Maintenance Initiative allocation handover ceremony at the Che Luan Khor Moral Uplifting Society here, in conjunction with the ministry's Sentuhan Kasih 2026 programme.
Nga also announced RM3.14 million in allocations for 27 RIBIs in Johor this year to support renovation, maintenance, new construction and emergency repairs.
He said the funding was aimed at ensuring worship facilities remained safe, functional and beneficial to their communities.
"The Madani government does not view development through the lens of race or religion alone, but ensures every community benefits fairly to strengthen national unity," he said.
To ensure transparency, Nga said the ministry would continue to closely monitor all approved projects so allocations are channelled appropriately.
He added that Johor had received RM18.75 million since 2023, benefiting 154 RIBIs in the state.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: KLIMS 2026 draws over 205,000 visitors
Article:
THE 11th edition of the Kuala Lumpur International Mobility Show (KLIMS 2026) wrapped up on a high note, attracting more than 205,440 visitors during its 10-day run at the Malaysia International Trade and Exhibition Centre.
Organised by the Malaysian Automotive Association (MAA) and managed by Qube Integrated Malaysia Sdn Bhd, the show carried the theme "Beyond Mobility", highlighting the automotive industry's shift towards electrification, greener technologies and intelligent mobility solutions.
Spanning seven halls, the event featured leading automotive brands including Proton, Perodua, Honda, Hyundai, Toyota, Mazda, Nissan, MG Motor, Xpeng, Zeekr and GWM, among others.
The show also generated strong commercial interest, with visitors actively participating in experiential activities such as the "Experience The Drive" programme and the Urban Play obstacle course.
MAA president Mohd Shamsor Mohd Zain said KLIMS has evolved beyond a traditional motor show.
"KLIMS 2026 continues to demonstrate the strength, resilience and adaptability of Malaysia's automotive industry.
"The strong participation from exhibitors and the overwhelming response from visitors reflect the industry's ongoing transformation towards electrification, innovation and smarter mobility solutions," he said.
He added that the event has become "a comprehensive mobility platform" that fosters collaboration, accelerates innovation and supports Malaysia's transition towards a safer, cleaner and more sustainable mobility future.
Among the headline unveilings were Proton's locally-assembled e.Mas5 and e.Mas 7 Premium Plus, Honda's Prelude hybrid coupe and Hyundai's high-performance Ioniq 5 N and Ioniq 6 N.
Visitors were also drawn to futuristic concepts including Perodua's C2 Concept EV sedan, Toyota's electrified Corolla concept and Xpeng's X2 flying car.
Qube executive chairman Richard Teo said KLIMS 2026 "has once again set a new benchmark in delivering a large-scale, immersive and engaging mobility showcase".
He said the event successfully combined automotive innovation, lifestyle experiences and community engagement, reinforcing KLIMS' standing as a regional automotive showcase of international standard.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Selangor Sultan's younger sister dies
Article:
SHAH ALAM: The younger sister of the Sultan of Selangor, Tengku Putri Arafiah Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah, died at her home in Section 12 today. She was 72.
A source from the Selangor Palace, confirming the news, said Tengku Putri Arafiah died at 10.15am due to age-related illness.
"The remains of Tengku Putri Arafiah will be taken to the Sultan Salahuddin Abdul Aziz Shah Mosque in Shah Alam, where funeral prayers will be held after Friday prayers.
"The burial is expected to take place at the Shah Alam Royal Mausoleum in Section 5 following the funeral prayers," the source said.
The Sultan of Selangor, Sultan Sharafuddin Idris Shah, is expected to attend the funeral prayers at the mosque.
The Tengku Permaisuri of Selangor, Tengku Permaisuri Norashikin, is also expected to attend. – BERNAMA
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johari proposes proportional representation to strengthen minority voice in Parliament
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Dewan Rakyat Speaker Tan Sri Johari Abdul has mooted the introduction of a proportional representation system, saying it could help develop a more inclusive generation of leaders that better reflects Malaysia's ethnic and cultural diversity.
He said such a system would prepare the country for shifting demographics while ensuring minority communities continue to have representation in Parliament.
Citing projections, Johari said that Bumiputera Malays were expected to make up 77 per cent of the population by 2050, raising questions over future minority representation.
"I'm convinced proportional representation is the best path forward. By 2050, Malay Bumiputeras will make up 77 per cent – so where will the minorities go? Where do they fit in? There won't be constituencies where they are the majority, and the likelihood of them having a voice in Parliament is getting smaller," he said.
"Think about it. If their voices are silenced, what happens on the ground? That is my concern," he said in his keynote address at the Harmony Symposium held at Parliament today.
Also present was Syahredzan Johan, chairman of the Malaysia Cross-Party Parliamentary Group on Racial and Religious Harmony (KRPPM-KKA).
Johari said discussions on national harmony must take a long-term view, extending beyond present-day concerns to future challenges over the next 50 to 100 years.
He said the focus should not be limited to current issues, adding that Malaysia was home to 77 ethnic groups, and stressing the importance of ensuring both majority and minority communities understand their roles in nation-building.
"Don't talk about today or yesterday. If we can, let's talk about tomorrow. Let us invest in the future. Yesterday is history," he said.
Meanwhile, Syahredzan, who is also Bangi MP, said the symposium aimed to bring discussions on racial and religious harmony into the heart of policymaking, with the goal of producing recommendations for Parliament and ministries.
He said KRPPM-KKA sought to advance a more inclusive Malaysia through policy and legal reforms, while strengthening cooperation between Parliament, government agencies, civil society and educational institutions.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: 19 roads to be closed or diverted for Johor nomination day tomorrow
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Several major roads near nomination centres across Johor will be closed or diverted in stages tomorrow in conjunction with nomination day for the 16th Johor election.
Johor police chief Datuk Ab Rahaman Arsad said the measures were to ensure safety and smooth traffic flow during the nomination process.
He said 19 roads in Johor Baru, Kulai, Batu Pahat, Muar and Kluang had been identified for closures or diversions.
Among them are Jalan Utama Dato' Onn and Jalan Ayer Molek in Johor Bahru, Jalan Persiaran Indahpura 6 in Kulai, Jalan Omar in Batu Pahat, Jalan Arab and Jalan Petri in Muar, as well as Jalan Dato' Teoh Siew Khor and Jalan Sultan in Kluang.
He said details on closure times and diversions would be announced from time to time through official police channels.
Ab Rahaman said 22 locations in 17 state constituencies had also been placed under close monitoring based on security assessments and past election experience.
He said these included nomination centres expected to draw large crowds of supporters, as well as busy urban areas with heavy traffic.
A total of 4,832 police personnel will be deployed on nomination day to maintain security, manage traffic, monitor public order and enforce the law.
The public are advised to plan their journeys early, follow instructions from authorities and use alternative routes where necessary.
Ab Rahaman also reminded political supporters to act in a disciplined manner, comply with the law and avoid provocation or gatherings that could disrupt public order.
He said cooperation from all parties was crucial to ensure the nomination process proceeded smoothly in a peaceful and orderly environment.
Nomination day for the state election is tomorrow, followed by early voting on July 7 and polling on July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Cockroaches, rats: DBKL issues 74 notices, shuts six Jalan Alor eateries
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Kuala Lumpur City Hall (DBKL) issued 74 notices and compounds against food premises during a special enforcement operation around Jalan Alor, Bukit Bintang, yesterday.
The operation, carried out jointly with the Health Ministry (MOH), involved inspections at 45 food premises.
"The operation was aimed at ensuring hygiene standards, food safety and compliance with licensing requirements," DBKL said in a Facebook statement.
Among the offences detected were poor cleanliness, failure to install or properly maintain grease traps, and the presence of cockroaches and rats in kitchen areas.
Inspectors also found food items being stored improperly, workers who had not received typhoid vaccinations or attended mandatory food handling courses, as well as breaches of smoking and licensing regulations.
The notices and compounds were issued under several laws, including the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur Food Establishment Licensing By-Laws 2016, the Federal Territory Food Handlers By-Laws 1979, the Street, Drainage and Building Act 1974, the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur Trade, Business and Industry Licensing By-Laws 2016, and the Control of Smoking Products for Public Health Act 2024.
Meanwhile, six food premises were ordered to close under By-Law 28(1) of the Federal Territory of Kuala Lumpur Food Establishment Licensing By-Laws 2016 for failing to comply with prescribed hygiene requirements.
DBKL said enforcement operations would continue at food premises across the capital to ensure cleanliness, food safety and the well-being of city residents.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Syed Saddiq leaves fate to God as Federal Court ruling nears
Article:
PETALING JAYA: Muar MP Syed Saddiq Syed Abdul Rahman says he is leaving his fate to God as he awaits the Federal Court's decision on the prosecution's final appeal in his case, due in four days.
The former youth and sports minister said he remained calm ahead of the ruling and confident in the judicial process he had gone through for seven years.
"I am confident that I am on the side of truth and have gone through the judicial process for seven years. Throughout this period, I have never sought to delay the case or have it dropped, nor have I pursued any political avenue of appeal.
"I also want to send a clear message to young people not to fear adversity. We must remain principled and uphold our dignity because the struggle goes beyond any individual," he said after a special prayer session held for him last night.
"I am ready, and I leave everything to God," he added.
The former Muda president, is facing charges of criminal breach of trust, dishonest misappropriation of property and money laundering involving Bersatu Youth (Armada) funds.
The prosecution's final appeal against his acquittal will be decided on Tuesday.
On June 25 last year, the Court of Appeal acquitted and discharged him after allowing his appeal against the High Court's Nov 9, 2023 conviction, which had sentenced him to seven years' jail, two strokes of the cane and a RM10 million fine.
A day later, the Attorney-General's Chambers filed an appeal at the Federal Court.
Syed Saddiq was previously charged with abetting Rafiq Hakim Razali, then Armada assistant treasurer, in criminal breach of trust involving RM1 million in Armada funds at CIMB Bank, KL Sentral, on March 6, 2020.
He also faces a charge of dishonest misappropriation of RM120,000 belonging to Armada Bumi Bersatu Enterprise, as well as two counts of money laundering involving RM50,000 transferred into his Amanah Saham Bumiputera account.
Asked whether the court decision would affect his wedding plans, Syed Saddiq said it was too early to say.
"It is difficult for me to say whether situation A or B will happen. Let us wait for the decision, but have faith and place your trust in God," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: RTM, Astro strengthen partnership to expand broadcast reach
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Radio Television Malaysia (RTM) and Astro have agreed to strengthen strategic cooperation to widen access to quality content across multiple broadcasting platforms.
In a joint statement, both parties said the expanded collaboration would see RTM channels made more widely available on Astro, including Sukan+, Berita RTM and RTM's radio network.
Malaysians will continue to have access to TV1, TV2 and TV Okey via Astro, NJOI and Sooka platforms.
The expanded lineup, including Berita RTM, Sukan+ and RTM radio stations, will be made available to Astro customers in the near future.
They said the initiative was expected to broaden public access to trusted and relevant content while strengthening the dissemination of information in the national interest.
RTM and Astro added that they remained committed to expanding cooperation to accelerate local content development, strengthen creative talent, and support a more sustainable and competitive media industry.
They said the current media landscape called for a more progressive and inclusive approach in line with evolving technology and changing audience consumption patterns.
Both parties expressed confidence that the strengthened partnership would benefit the industry and expand access to high-quality, credible content for Malaysians.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: APHM working with govt to tackle rising medical costs
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: The Association of Private Hospitals Malaysia (APHM) says it is working closely with the government to address rising medical costs through its role in the Joint Ministerial Committee on Private Healthcare Costs (JMCPHC).
Its president, Datuk Dr Kuljit Singh, said the association supported initiatives by the Health Ministry and Finance Ministry to tackle medical cost inflation, including issues raised in the Public Accounts Committee (PAC) report, many of which are already being addressed through the committee.
He said APHM, as an industry representative on the JMCPHC, had also engaged the Health Ministry to improve hospital bill transparency, an area currently being developed in collaboration with stakeholders.
"APHM continues to support broader efforts of the JMCPHC to manage rising healthcare costs by contributing industry data and providing constructive input to inform policy development," he said in a statement.
Dr Kuljit said the association remained committed to patient care and to working with the government at national level to develop evidence-based and sustainable solutions that ensure the long-term affordability and quality of healthcare in the country.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Mahfuz: Pas must explain backing for Bersatu despite allegations
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: An Amanah leader has questioned Pas over its continued backing of Bersatu candidates in the Johor election despite what he described as serious allegations made by the party against its Perikatan Nasional (PN) ally.
Amanah vice-president Mahfuz Omar said Pas leaders had previously accused Bersatu of betrayal in Perlis, undermining political efforts in Negri Sembilan, violating syariah principles in political cooperation, weakening unity within PN, being greedy in seat negotiations, lacking grassroots machinery and taking advantage of Pas.
He said the allegations went beyond routine political disagreements as they involved questions of integrity, trust and Islamic principles that Pas claims to uphold.
"Johor voters are now faced with a major question. If these allegations are true, why is Pas still allowing Bersatu to contest 16 seats in the Johor election under the PN banner?
"Why were Bersatu candidates' appointment letters still signed by PN chairman Datuk Seri Dr Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar, who is also Pas vice-president?
"Why is Pas still asking its members and supporters to campaign for Bersatu candidates?" he said.
Mahfuz also questioned why Bersatu was allocated the largest number of seats despite the allegations.
Under the seat distribution, Pas will contest 11 seats, the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) four, while Parti Pejuang Tanah Air – now part of PN – will contest one seat. Bersatu has been allocated 16 seats.
He said Pas members and Johor voters deserved clarity on whether the allegations were based on principle or reflected an internal struggle for influence within PN.
"If Pas truly believes Bersatu has betrayed the struggle and violated syariah principles, the consistent course of action would have been to remove Bersatu from PN or prevent it from contesting under the coalition's banner," he said.
Instead, Mahfuz said Pas continued to rely on Bersatu in the state election while maintaining serious accusations against the party, raising questions over the coalition's credibility and sincerity.
He also questioned whether a coalition marked by internal accusations and mistrust could effectively govern Johor.
"The Johor election is not merely about seat allocation. It is about public confidence in a coalition's ability to lead the state.
"What PN is showing today is a coalition that is increasingly divided, inconsistent and unable to explain its own position to the people," he said.
Mahfuz said if Pas still intended to work with Bersatu, it should explain why its earlier allegations did not prevent it from giving the party the largest share of seats.
He said that voters deserved a clear explanation, saying they should not be expected to support a coalition that accused one of its own partners of violating syariah principles while simultaneously urging support for the same party.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Musk's SpaceX targets US consumers with Starlink mobile service push
Article:
CALIFORNIA: SpaceX has told investors it plans to launch a Starlink mobile service for US consumers, the Financial Times reported on Friday, in a move that could allow the Elon Musk-led company to compete directly with Verizon , AT&T and T-Mobile.
SpaceX already offers direct-to-cell connectivity with T-Mobile in the US, providing supplemental coverage from space to extend internet access to remote areas.
SpaceX is now considering launching a Starlink retail product and could build its own terrestrial US mobile network, President Gwynne Shotwell told investors during a recent IPO roadshow, the FT report said, citing sources.
Reuters could not immediately verify the report. SpaceX did not immediately respond to a Reuters request for comment outside regular business hours.
In September last year, SpaceX bought wireless spectrum licenses from EchoStar for its Starlink satellite network for about US$17 billion and then again for US$2.6 billion in November, giving it the ability to quickly create a strong and affordable direct-to-cell service by using EchoStar's wireless airwaves.
SpaceX will disrupt the US$1.6 trillion US communications industry as its satellite broadband unit Starlink expands, brokerage firm ​Oppenheimer said in a note earlier this month.
SpaceX's record valuation is grounded in Starlink, which has over 10 million subscribers, and a launch business that analysts and investors say has transformed access to orbit.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Isa Samad conviction review: Federal Court to decide fate after Friday prayers
Article:
PUTRAJAYA: Datuk Mohd Isa Abdul Samad will know after Friday prayers today whether the Federal Court will allow his bid to review its decision reinstating his six-year jail sentence and RM15.45 million fine for corruption.
A three-member Federal Court bench chaired by Chief Judge of Malaya Datuk Seri Hashim Hamzah reserved its decision until 3pm after hearing lengthy submissions from both parties this morning.
The former Felda chairman is seeking leave under Rule 137 of the Rules of the Federal Court 1995 to review the apex court's Feb 10 decision, claiming there was a breach of natural justice.
He contends that the previous Federal Court panel did not give the defence an opportunity to submit arguments on the merits of the appeal and in mitigation before reinstating his conviction and sentence.
Mohd Isa, 77, has been serving his six-year prison sentence at Sungai Buloh Prison since Feb 10 after the Federal Court allowed the prosecution's appeal and reinstated the High Court's conviction, sentence and fine.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Palm oil rises tracking Dalian, but on track for a weekly loss
Article:
JAKARTA: Malaysian palm oil futures rose on Friday, tracking strength in rival vegetable oils in the Dalian market, although futures were on track for a weekly loss.
The benchmark palm oil contract for September delivery on the Bursa Malaysia Derivatives Exchange was up RM50, or 1.1 per cent, at RM4,607 (US$1,122.84) per metric ton in early trade.
The futures has booked a 1.10 per cent weekly loss so far.
Dalian's most-active soyoil contract rose 0.87 per cent while its palm oil contract gained 1.3 per cent. Soyoil prices on the Chicago Board of Trade fell 0.28 per cent
Palm oil tracks the price movements of rival edible oils, as it competes for a share of the global vegetable oils market.
According to independent inspection company AmSpec Agri Malaysia, exports of Malaysian palm oil products for June 1 to 25 rose 11.1 per cent, while according to cargo surveyor Intertek Testing Services it rose 10.6 per cent.
Meanwhile, Indonesia has issued a regulation to implement its B50 biodiesel mandate starting from July 1, with a three-month transition period for retailers to clear their existing stocks, a senior energy ministry official said on Thursday.
Oil prices fell on Friday morning and are heading for steep weekly losses amid easing supply concerns as more stranded oil tankers exited the Strait of Hormuz, even though a cargo vessel was hit near Oman on Thursday.
Weaker crude oil futures make palm a less attractive option for biodiesel feedstock.
Palm oil FCPOc3 may revisit its June 23 high of RM4,710 per metric ton and fall toward RM4,542, Reuters technical analyst Wang Tao said.
Asian shares eased from record highs on Friday as hefty price hikes from Apple showed the downside of the boom in chip demand, while only the threat of Japanese intervention kept the yen from hitting 40-year lows.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Kioxia shares slump 12pct as AI-related stocks fall
Article:
TOKYO: Shares of Japanese chipmaker Kioxia slid 12 per cent on Friday after a report that ChatGPT maker OpenAI was considering delaying its initial public offering sparked a selloff in AI-related shares.
Kioxia, previously called Toshiba Memory and carved out of Toshiba in 2018, is a major producer of memory chips. Its shares have surged as AI investment has boosted the chip industry, making it the most valuable company on the Nikkei 225 index.
But on Friday it was hit by a broader selloff after the New York Times reported that OpenAI is considering holding off on its IPO until next year as CEO Sam Altman seeks a US$1 trillion valuation. Kioxia said on Thursday it is considering a stock split and aims to list American depositary shares on a US exchange at the beginning of the next financial year, which runs until March 2028.
"Whether it's April, May, or June is not yet clear, but we're hoping to list... around that time," Chief Financial Officer Yoshihiko Kawamura said at Kioxia's annual general meeting.
Asian tech firms are looking to expand their investor base in the US, with chipmaker SK Hynix saying this week it plans to raise up to US$29.4 billion through a US listing.
"The timeframe to complete this offering suggests that (Kioxia) is highly confident of its ability to continue to produce outstanding results in the next 9-12 months," analyst Douglas Kim wrote on the Smartkarma platform.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Johor polls: BN, PH, PN set for clashes in 33 of 56 state seats
Article:
JOHOR BARU: Barisan Nasional (BN), Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Perikatan Nasional (PN) are expected to clash in 33 of the 56 state seats in the Johor election next month.
BN and PH are contesting all 56 seats, while PN has distributed its 33 seats among Bersatu, Pas, Pejuang and the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP).
Based on the full list of candidates announced separately by PN components last night, Bersatu will contest 16 seats, Pas 11, MIPP five and Pejuang one seat in Gambir.
The 16 seats seeing direct three-cornered fights between BN, PH and Bersatu are Kemelah, Bukit Kepong, Bukit Pasir, Serom, Bukit Naning, Semarang, Parit Raja, Senggarang, Rengit, Layang-Layang, Tenggaroh, Panti, Sedili, Johor Lama, Penawar and Stulang.
The 11 seats involving BN, PH and PAS are Jementah, Tenang, Simpang Jeram, Maharani, Sungai Balang, Semerah, Sri Medan, Kahang, Endau, Pasir Raja and Tiram.
Another five seats will see BN, PH and MIPP in three-cornered contests, namely Pemanis, Paloh, Permas, Kota Iskandar and Bukit Permai.
Several "hot seats" are expected to draw intense attention, including Puteri Wangsa, Endau, Machap and Bukit Kepong.
In Puteri Wangsa, former education minister Dr Maszlee Malik (PH) will face BN and PN candidates in a three-cornered fight.
Endau is also in focus as former incumbent Alwiyah Talib returns to contest under Umno after leaving Bersatu earlier this month.
Machap, held by caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi, will also be closely watched.
Bukit Kepong, currently held by Johor Bersatu chief and former menteri besar Datuk Dr Sahruddin Jamal, remains another key battleground.
Skudai is also expected to draw attention, with BN, PH and PN in the race, alongside the Socialist Party of Malaysia (PSM), which is fielding its sole candidate in the state election.
Separately, new party Bersama will contest 15 seats, including Bukit Naning, Mahkota, Tiram, Puteri Wangsa, Johor Jaya, Permas, Larkin, Stulang, Perling, Kempas, Skudai, Kota Iskandar, Bukit Permai, Bukit Batu and Senai.
Of these, seven seats are currently held by Umno, five by DAP, and one each by PKR and Muda.
All Bersama candidates are expected to be announced at Kampung Melayu Majidee tonight.
The Johor state assembly was dissolved on June 1 following consent from the Regent of Johor Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim.
In the 2022 state election, BN formed the state government after winning 40 seats, while PH secured 12, PN three and Muda one.
Nomination day is tomorrow, followed by early voting on July 7, with polling set for July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Pas says it will not help Bersatu campaign in Johor polls despite PN banner
Article:
MARANG: Pas will not deploy its election machinery to help Bersatu campaign in the 16th Johor state election, despite both parties contesting under the Perikatan Nasional (PN) banner.
Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said the two parties would use separate machinery and campaign strategies, although they would share the PN logo in the July 11 polls.
"No... the campaigns are different because they (Bersatu) insist on using the logo and we are not breaking up the PN alliance. We remain with PN. As for Bersatu, we do not know their strategy.
"Perhaps they have their own approach. The Pas machinery will focus on our 11 state seats," he told reporters after delivering a Duha lecture at Masjid Rusila today.
Hadi also said the dispute between Pas and Bersatu had been resolved, adding that the party stood by its decision to end political cooperation with Bersatu.
He said Pas aims to strengthen its role as the opposition and serve as a check and balance on the Johor government.
"We are contesting 11 seats. We have chosen constituencies we are confident of winning. The important thing is that Johor is governed by Malay Muslims," he said.
Nomination for the Johor election is tomorrow, with early voting on July 7 and polling on July 11.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Labour policies must address underemployment, skills mismatch — MEF
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR: Future labour market interventions should focus not only on unemployment but also on underemployment, skills mismatches and workers employed below their qualifications, says the Malaysian Employers Federation (MEF).
Its president Datuk Dr Syed Hussain Syed Husman said while Malaysia has one of the region's most comprehensive support systems for displaced workers, the next priority should be improving workforce adaptability.
"Employers stand ready to work closely with the government to create more employment opportunities and strengthen workforce competitiveness," he said in a statement today.
Syed Hussain called for stronger collaboration between the government, employers and workers to build a more resilient labour market that supports sustainable, meaningful employment.
He also welcomed the government's continued commitment to helping displaced workers return to work through labour market institutions, social protection mechanisms, and reskilling and upskilling programmes.
"Recent reports of job losses have understandably raised concerns. However, Malaysia remains among the few countries in the region with a comprehensive and coordinated support framework for displaced workers," he said.
He added that the ecosystem, led by the Human Resources Ministry, the Social Security Organisation (Perkeso), the Employment Insurance System (EIS), Human Resource Development Corporation (HRD Corp) and Talent Corporation Malaysia (TalentCorp), reflected the government's recognition that unemployment is both an economic and social issue.
"Many countries do not have the breadth of institutional support available in Malaysia. The existence of dedicated agencies for social protection, employment services, workforce development and talent management reflects the seriousness with which the government addresses labour market challenges," he said.
© New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

Title: Six children among 10 hurt in three-vehicle crash near Bentong
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Six children were among 10 people injured in a three-vehicle crash along Jalan Mempaga–Raub near Kem Smart BKERP in Kampung Lebu, Bentong, today.
Pahang Fire and Rescue Department (JBPM) assistant director of operations Mohd Salahuddin Isa said two victims suffered relatively serious injuries to their waist and legs, while two other occupants of one of the vehicles escaped unhurt.
The crash, reported at about 11am, involved a Perodua Myvi, Perodua Viva and Perodua Bezza.
Mohd Salahuddin said the victims comprised three men, three women and six children, including three girls. None was trapped in the wreckage.
“Initial treatment was provided to the injured victims before the arrival of medical personnel,” he said in a statement.
“The two victims who sustained relatively serious injuries were then handed over to the medical team to be taken to hospital for further treatment.”
Firefighters from the Karak Fire and Rescue Station, assisted by personnel from the Raub station, were dispatched to the scene, about 40 kilometres away.
They later cleared debris from the road before concluding the operation at 12.42pm.

Title: EC: 593 nomination forms sold ahead of Johor polls, but only 133 candidates pay deposits
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — A total of 593 nomination forms have been sold for the Johor state election so far, but only 133 prospective candidates have formally confirmed their participation by paying the required election deposit, the Election Commission (EC) said today.
EC chairman Datuk Seri Ramlan Harun said the number is expected to increase as prospective candidates still have until nomination proceedings begin tomorrow morning to complete the necessary formalities.
“As of this morning, we have sold 593 nomination forms at Returning Officer (RO) offices throughout the state. Of that number, 133 candidates have made their deposit payments, meaning they have confirmed their participation so far.
“We still have time until tomorrow, and we hope candidates intending to contest will come early to make their payments so that the nomination process can proceed more smoothly,” he told reporters after inspecting preparations at the nomination centre for the Maharani state constituency at the Muar District Office today.
Ramlan also assured that all 56 nomination centres across Johor are fully prepared after the EC conducted two consecutive days of trial runs.
On security, he reminded all political parties, candidates and supporters to comply with election regulations and avoid any form of provocation to ensure nomination day proceeds peacefully and orderly.
Meanwhile, Maharani Returning Officer Zainal Eran said supporters from political parties attending tomorrow's nomination process will be placed in designated areas separated by barricades to prevent any untoward incidents.
“Only the candidate, proposer and one supporter will be allowed into the nomination centre under the prescribed controls.
“Other supporters will be placed at the field in front of the centre, where barriers will be installed to separate supporters of each party to prevent any undesirable incidents,” he said.
So far, Pakatan Harapan (PH) has fielded candidates in all 56 state seats, comprising 20 from PKR, 19 from Amanah and 17 from DAP.
Barisan Nasional (BN) is also contesting all 56 seats with 36 Umno candidates, 16 from MCA and four from MIC.
Perikatan Nasional (PN) has announced that PAS will contest 11 seats, Bersatu 16, the Malaysian Indian People's Party (MIPP) five and Pejuang one.
Meanwhile, the Malaysian United Democratic Alliance (Muda) will contest four seats, Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) one seat, while Parti Bersama Malaysia (Bersama) is expected to make its electoral debut by contesting 15 state seats.
The Johor State Legislative Assembly was dissolved on June 1. The EC has set tomorrow as nomination day, July 7 for early voting and July 11 for polling day. — Bernama

Title: Anwar says no directive issued to stop Onn Hafiz entering Felda settlements
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today denied claims that anyone had been instructed to prevent Johor caretaker Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi from entering Felda settlements, saying the federal government only handles matters under its own jurisdiction.
According to
Berita Harian
, Anwar said there had been no directive to bar Onn Hafiz from entering Felda areas after the Johor leader claimed he had been prevented from attending a land title presentation ceremony for settlers.
“There was no such instruction (to block Onn Hafiz). The federal government handles federal projects.
“The state government carries out state matters. Why make such a fuss about it?” he told reporters after performing Friday prayers at Masjid Jameatus Solehah in Dengkil today.
Anwar was responding to Onn Hafiz’s allegation that he had been prevented from entering a Felda settlement to present land titles to settlers during the Johor State Felda Settlers’ Day 2026 celebration.
According to Onn Hafiz, the event, originally scheduled to be held at Felda Ulu Belitong in Kluang on June 20 and 21, was moved to Dewan Dato’ Onn in Taman Sri Lambak after he was not allowed to enter the settlement.
Speaking at the rescheduled event, Onn Hafiz said he was grateful the ceremony was able to proceed despite the delay.
“It turns out that I wasn’t even allowed to enter a Felda settlement to present land titles to settlers. This is the first time in my life in Johor that a menteri besar has not been allowed into a Felda area.
“This is not the manager’s fault. I think we can organise another special programme to present the settlers with their land titles,” he was quoted as saying, according to the national daily.
Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi, who oversees rural development, had also earlier denied issuing any directive to prevent the Johor caretaker menteri besar from entering Felda areas.

Title: New low-rent housing projects planned for civil servants nationwide, says Anwar
Article:
DENGKIL, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim said the government will develop housing for civil servants with lower rental rates as an effort to look after the welfare of the group.
Anwar, who is also the finance minister, said this is because he found that civil servants were facing serious problems with house rent during his visit to several states such as Penang, Perak, Johor and Negeri Sembilan.
“We will develop housing for civil servants with cheap rent.
“So it will not burden the family. These are the steps we are taking, we will expedite it,” he told reporters after performing Friday prayers at Jameatus Solehah Mosque here today.
Anwar said that although the government had increased the salaries of civil servants, the issue of increasing rental rates is still a problem in several states.
“Even though we increase their salaries by between 15 (to) a maximum of 30 per cent, sometimes in Johor Bahru, Kuala Lumpur, Seremban, Ipoh, they cannot cover the increased rent,” he said.
He said government-owned land had been identified to be used for the development of the housing.
“Whether for customs or the police, as soon as there is space, we will develop housing for civil servants,” he said.
Earlier, Anwar greeted about 1,200 congregants who performed Friday prayers at the mosque, led by Muhammad Asyraf Kamaluddin. — Bernama

Title: Nine arrested as GOF uncovers RM3.75m illegal bauxite operation in Felda plantation
Article:
KUANTAN, June 26 — The General Operations Force (GOF) detained nine individuals and seized assets and minerals worth an estimated RM3.75 million in a coordinated Op Khazanah at an oil palm plantation in Felda Bukit Goh here yesterday.
Southeast Brigade GOF commander SAC Ahmad Radzi Hussain said the 11.30am operation led to the arrest of four local men and five foreign men believed to be involved in illegal bauxite mining activities.
“Preliminary investigations found that the five foreign nationals failed to produce valid work permits and identification documents. They have been handed over to the Kuantan District Police CID for further action,” he said in a statement today.
The operation involved the intelligence branch of the 7th Battalion GOF Kuantan, the Pahang State Enforcement Unit (UPNP), and the Criminal Investigation Division of the Kuantan District Police Headquarters.
He said the four locals and the seized items were handed over to UPNP for further investigation.
Ahmad Radzi said the seizure included three dumper trucks, two excavators, two piles of soil believed to contain about 10,000 tonnes of bauxite, and six documents, with the total value estimated at RM3.75 million.
He added that UPNP also collected soil samples to be sent to the Minerals and Geoscience Department for analysis, and ordered an immediate shutdown of the site.
Preliminary findings showed the site was owned by a Felda settler and had been leased to a company since March for land clearing and replanting works.
He said the minerals were believed to have been sent to a processing plant in Gebeng before being sold to buyers.
Police are also tracking down the employer suspected of hiring foreign workers without valid work permits to assist in the investigation.
The case is being investigated under Section 158 of the Pahang Mineral Enactment 2001, as well as Sections 15(1)(c), 39(b) and 6(1)(c) of the Immigration Act 1959/63. — Bernama

Title: Maserati, Alphard, Rolexes and RM6.3m in drugs seized in crackdown on alleged ‘Datuk’ kingpin
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — A man holding the title ‘Datuk’, believed to be the mastermind of a drug syndicate, was among 291 individuals detained in Op Gaurus conducted by the Bukit Aman Narcotics Criminal Investigation Department (JSJN) across Perak, the Klang Valley and Johor last week.
Bukit Aman NCIID director Datuk Hussein Omar Khan said the suspect, in his mid-40s, is believed to be the leader of a secret society active in the states.
He said that in the operation carried out from June 17 to 21, police seized various types of drugs, including heroin, MDMA, ketamine, ecstasy pills, Erimin 5 pills and marijuana, with an estimated value of RM6.26 million, and arrested nine syndicate members.
“The modus operandi of the syndicate is to purchase drugs in bulk before repackaging them for sale at entertainment outlets.
“This syndicate is believed to have been active in Perak, the Klang Valley and Johor since mid-2024, and also possessed various equipment and precursor chemicals used to process drugs,” he said at a press conference here today.
Hussein said several vehicles, including a Nissan Sentra, Mazda, Maserati Ghibli, Toyota Alphard, Toyota Vellfire and Mercedes-Benz CL250, as well as two Rolex watches and RM4,383 in cash, were also seized in the operation.
He said the suspects, aged between 19 and 74, had been remanded, with five of them testing positive for drugs, and the case was being investigated under the Dangerous Drugs Act 1952. — Bernama

Title: Anwar says top approval rating is no excuse for complacency, vows to work harder
Article:
KUALA LUMPUR, June 26 — Prime Minister Datuk Seri Anwar Ibrahim today said being named Malaysia’s most popular political leader should not make him complacent, insisting that public approval must instead spur him to improve his government’s performance.
According to
Utusan Malaysia
, Anwar said recognition from the public should serve as motivation to deliver better results rather than an excuse to ease up.
“Instead, it (the recognition) should serve as a catalyst to continue improving our performance and working even harder for the people.
Alhamdulillah
, I’ve heard about those findings,
Alhamdulillah
,” he was quoted as saying after Friday prayers at Masjid Jameatus Solehah in Dengkil.
“But I always say, in the spirit of
islah ma istata’tu
(making improvements to the best of our ability), that we must continue striving to improve our performance.
“We must always try to do our best, work harder and never become carried away by praise.”
Anwar was responding to a Merdeka Center survey released yesterday that gave him the highest approval rating among Malaysia’s leading political figures at 52 per cent.
The poll placed returning Umno leader Khairy Jamaluddin second with a 50 per cent approval rating, followed by former prime minister Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin at 36 per cent.
Former economy minister Datuk Seri Rafizi Ramli, who has since left PKR to form Bersama, recorded 32 per cent, while Perikatan Nasional chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Samsuri Mokhtar garnered 28 per cent.
PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang and Umno president and Deputy Prime Minister Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi registered approval ratings of 25 per cent and 24 per cent respectively.
The survey also found that 42 per cent of respondents believed Malaysia was heading in the right direction, unchanged from similar polls conducted in December 2025 and February 2026, while half said the country was moving in the wrong direction.
Economic issues remained voters’ biggest concern, with 73 per cent identifying the economy, cost of living and fuel prices as the country’s most pressing challenge.
Conducted between March 12 and April 9, the survey involved 1,209 registered voters and was designed to reflect Malaysia’s national electoral profile.

Title: Four denied bail over alleged Jelutong temple attack sparked by false theft claim
Article:
GEORGE TOWN, June 26 — Four men pleaded not guilty in the Sessions Court here today to charges of being members of an unlawful assembly with the common intention of causing grievous hurt to a man last week.
The accused, R. Nahveen Raaj, 34; S. Logakumaran, 31; M. Sanjit Kumar, 38; and K. Dineskumar, 30, were jointly charged with four other individuals who remain at large.
They were accused of being members of an unlawful assembly in which one or more members allegedly caused grievous hurt to Mohamed Umar Sheriff Mohamed Ameen, 31.
The offence allegedly took place at the Hean Seng Kong Temple in Jelutong here at 4.24am on June 21.
The charge was framed under Section 149 of the Penal Code, which provides for imprisonment of up to 20 years, and shall also be liable to a fine or caning, upon conviction.
The prosecution was conducted by deputy public prosecutor Lee Jun Keong, while all the accused were represented by lawyer V. Parthipan.
Citing the severity of the victim’s injuries and the risk of the accused absconding, Judge Irwan Suainbon denied bail to all four men.
The court has scheduled August 28 for the submission of the full medical report.
On Tuesday, police arrested six men suspected of wounding two men with sharp weapons at a temple in Jelutong, in the early hours of last Sunday.
Penang police chief Datuk Dennis Lim Kwang Keng said the incident is believed to have stemmed from a misunderstanding at an entertainment outlet, when one of the victims, aged 31, was accused of stealing a suspect’s mobile phone, an allegation that turned out to be false. — Bernama

Title: MetMalaysia: Rain forecast across much of Johor on state election nomination day
Article:
JOHOR BAHRU, June 26 — Rain is forecast to affect seven of the 10 districts in the state tomorrow morning, potentially posing a slight “challenge” for prospective candidates and supporters on nomination day for the Johor state election.
Johor director of the Malaysian Meteorological Department (MetMalaysia), Azlai Ta’at, said rain is expected in Johor Bahru, Muar, Batu Pahat, Kota Tinggi, Pontian, Tangkak and Kulai.
However, clear weather is expected in the morning in the other three districts of Segamat, Kluang and Mersing.
“Minimum temperatures across the state are expected to range between 24 and 25°C, while maximum temperatures will be between 31 and 34°C, with Segamat recording the highest at up to 34°C.
“The public, including supporters of contesting candidates, are advised to stay alert to any changes in weather conditions,” he told Bernama here today.
Thunderstorms are also expected to mark the start of the campaigning period, which will officially begin immediately after the nomination process ends tomorrow.
MetMalaysia forecast thunderstorms in Johor Bahru, Segamat, Kluang, Kota Tinggi, Pontian, Mersing and Kulai in the afternoon, while Batu Pahat and Tangkak are expected to experience rain, and Muar is forecast to be hot.
The nomination process is scheduled to take place from 9am to 10am at 56 nomination centres, before the official announcement of eligible candidates is made by returning officers.
Pakatan Harapan is fielding candidates in all 56 seats, involving 20 from PKR, 19 from Amanah and 17 from DAP.
Barisan Nasional is also contesting 56 seats, fielding 36 UMNO candidates, 16 MCA candidates and four MIC candidates.
Perikatan Nasional has announced that PAS will contest 11 seats, Bersatu 16, the Malaysian Indian People’s Party five and Pejuang one.
The Malaysian United Democratic Alliance will contest four seats, Parti Sosialis Malaysia one, while Parti Bersama Malaysia is set to make its debut by contesting 15 seats.
The Election Commission (EC) has set July 7 for early voting and July 11 as polling day.
